# MCP Echo Server Timestamp Tests
# Tests timestamp functionality and format validation

# Build the echo server binary
go build -o mcp-echo-server .

# Test timestamp format in response
exec mcp-echo-server <<EOF
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2024-11-05","capabilities":{}},"id":1}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialized","params":{}}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"echo","arguments":{"message":"timestamp test"}},"id":2}
EOF

# Verify timestamp is in RFC3339 format (e.g., 2024-01-15T14:30:45Z)
stdout '"echo":"timestamp test"'
stdout '"timestamp":"[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}'

# Test multiple calls have different timestamps (or at least not identical)
exec mcp-echo-server <<EOF
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2024-11-05","capabilities":{}},"id":1}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialized","params":{}}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"echo","arguments":{"message":"first"}},"id":2}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"echo","arguments":{"message":"second"}},"id":3}
EOF

stdout '"echo":"first"'
stdout '"echo":"second"'
stdout '"timestamp"'

# Test that timestamp is included in every response
exec mcp-echo-server <<EOF
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2024-11-05","capabilities":{}},"id":1}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialized","params":{}}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"echo","arguments":{"message":"a"}},"id":2}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"echo","arguments":{"message":"b"}},"id":3}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"echo","arguments":{"message":"c"}},"id":4}
EOF

stdout '"echo":"a".*"timestamp"'
stdout '"echo":"b".*"timestamp"'
stdout '"echo":"c".*"timestamp"'

# Test timestamp with Unicode message
exec mcp-echo-server <<EOF
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2024-11-05","capabilities":{}},"id":1}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialized","params":{}}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"echo","arguments":{"message":"Unicode test: 🕐🕑🕒⏰"}},"id":2}
EOF

stdout '"echo":"Unicode test: 🕐🕑🕒⏰"'
stdout '"timestamp":"[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}'

# Test timestamp with very long message
exec mcp-echo-server <<EOF
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2024-11-05","capabilities":{}},"id":1}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialized","params":{}}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"echo","arguments":{"message":"This is a very long message that tests whether the timestamp is still properly included even when the message content is quite lengthy and spans multiple words and contains various punctuation marks and numbers like 123 and 456."}},"id":2}
EOF

stdout '"timestamp":"[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}'

# Test timestamp precision (should include seconds)
exec mcp-echo-server <<EOF
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2024-11-05","capabilities":{}},"id":1}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialized","params":{}}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"echo","arguments":{"message":"precision test"}},"id":2}
EOF

# Should have at least seconds precision
stdout '"timestamp":"[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}'

# Test that timestamp field comes after echo field in JSON
exec mcp-echo-server <<EOF
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2024-11-05","capabilities":{}},"id":1}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialized","params":{}}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"echo","arguments":{"message":"order test"}},"id":2}
EOF

stdout '"echo":"order test","timestamp"'

# Test that both echo and timestamp are always present
exec mcp-echo-server <<EOF
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2024-11-05","capabilities":{}},"id":1}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialized","params":{}}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"echo","arguments":{"message":"completeness test"}},"id":2}
EOF

stdout '"echo":"completeness test"'
stdout '"timestamp"'
# Both fields should be in the same response
stdout '"echo":"completeness test".*"timestamp"'
